blocks.text: SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK

You are hereby summoned to answer the complaint in this action, and to serve a copy of your answer, or if the complaint is not served with this summons, to serve a notice of appearance on the Plaintiff’s attorney(s) within twenty days after the service of this summons exclusive of the day of service, where service is made by delivery upon you personally within the state, or within 30 days after completion of service where service is made in any other manner. In case of your failure to appear or answer, judgment will be taken against you by default for the relief demanded in the complaint.
The basis of venue is Plaintiff’s Residence: 1070 Amity Road, Lot 52, in Bethany, New Haven County, Connecticut.
AS AND FOR A FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION:
1. At the time of the accident, Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA, was a lawful resident of 1070 Amity Road, Lot 52, Bethany, Connecticut 06524.
2. That on December 23, 2019, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, HENRY SARBIESKI, was a resident of 43 Mulberry Street, Naugatuck, Connecticut 06770.
3. That on December 23, 2019, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, HENRY SARBIESKI, was the owner of a 1995 Chevrolet motor vehicle bearing Connecticut license plate number DA3379.

4. That on December 23, 2019, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, HENRY SARBIESKI, was the registered owner of a 1995 Chevrolet motor vehicle bearing Connecticut license plate number DA3379.

5. That on December 23, 2019, at the aforesaid location, the 1995 Chevrolet motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number DA3379 owned and operated by Defendant, HENRY SARBIESKI, came in contact with the plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA.
6. That as a result of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA, was injured.
7. That as a result of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA, was seriously and permanently injured.

8. That the aforesaid contact and accident and the injuries resulting therefrom, were due solely and wholly as a result of the careless, reckless, and negligent manner in which the Defendant owned, operated and controlled the motor vehicle without the Plaintiff in any way contributing thereto.

9. The above-stated occurrence and the results thereof was caused by the negligence of the Defendant in the ownership, operation, management, maintenance, inspection, repair and control of said motor vehicle; in operating same without due regard to the rights and safety of the Plaintiff; in operating said motor vehicle in a manner which unreasonably endangered the Plaintiff; in failing to obey traffic signals; in failing to properly steer, guide, manage and control said vehicle, so as to prevent same from striking the plaintiff; in operating same at a rate of speed greater than was reasonable and proper at the time and place of the occurrence; in failing to apply the brakes or slow down or stop in such a manner as would have prevented the occurrence; in failing to have made adequate and timely observation of and response to conditions then existing at the place and time of the occurrence; in failing to observe signs and signals prevailing at the time and place of the occurrence; in failing to keep a proper look-out when operating and controlling said vehicle; in failing to properly maintain said motor vehicle; in failing to give adequate and timely signal notice or warning of the approach of his vehicle; in striking the plaintiff; in operating said motor vehicle in violation of the applicable vehicle and traffic laws, rules, regulations, statutes and ordinances of [accident_location_county] and State of [accident_location_state].
10. That the aforesaid occurrence and its results thereof were in no way due to any negligence or culpable conduct on the part of the Plaintiff.
11. That this action falls within one or more of the exception set forth in CPLR 1601 and CPLR 1602.

12. Pursuant to CPLR § 1602(2)(iv), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff’s damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff’s non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact Defendant owed the Plaintiff a non-delegable duty of care.

13. Plaintiff's damages, including, but not limited to Plaintiff's non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact that each of the answering Defendant is vicariously liable for the negligent acts and omissions of his agents, servants, or employees, which persons or entities may be one or more of the co-Defendant to this action.

14. Pursuant to CPLR § 1602(5), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff’s damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff's non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact that Defendant wrongful conduct was intentional.

15. Pursuant to CPLR § 1602(7), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff's damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff’s non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601 by reason of the fact that Defendant acted with reckless disregard for the safety of others.

16. Pursuant to CPLR § 1601(11), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff’s damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff’s non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact Defendant acted knowingly or intentionally, and in concert, to cause the acts or failures which are a proximate cause of Plaintiff’s injuries.

17. That by reason of the negligence of the Defendant as aforementioned, KELLEY SKAARVA, sustained serious bodily injuries, causing him great pain and discomfort in the past, present, and upon information and belief, in the future; he became and continues to be sick, sore, lame and disabled; upon information and belief, some of his injuries will be permanent; he was and upon information and belief, will be, compelled to seek medical attention for these injuries; and has been and upon information and belief, will be, unable to attend to his usual economic, educational and social activities.

18. By reason of the wrongful, negligent and unlawful actions of the Defendant, as aforesaid, the Plaintiff,KELLEY SKAARVA, sustained serious injuries as defined in §5102(d) of the Insurance Law of the State of [accident_location_state], and has sustained economic loss greater than basic economic loss as defined in said Insurance Law.

19. By reason of the foregoing and the negligence of said Defendant, the Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA is informed and verily believes, his aforesaid injuries are permanent and that he will permanently suffer from the effects of the aforesaid injuries and that he will be caused to suffer pain, suffering, significant limitations and consequential limitations of use of the affected body parts; and disruption of his pre-accident life.

20. By reason of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA, was compelled and did necessarily require medical aid and attention and did necessarily pay and become liable therefore, for medicines and upon information and belief, the Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA, will necessarily incur additional similar expenses.
21. By reason of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, KELLEY SKAARVA, has been unable to attend his usual occupation and vocation in the manner required.
22. That by reason of the foregoing, the Plaintiff has been damaged in sums, which exceeds the jurisdictional limits of all lower courts, which would otherwise have jurisdiction.
MICHAEL COHAN, an attorney duly admitted to practice before the Courts of the State of New York, affirms the following to be true under the penalties of perjury:

I am an attorney at COHAN LAW FIRM PLLC, attorneys of record for Plaintiff, Kelley Skaarva. I have read the annexed SUMMONS AND VERIFIED COMPLAINT and know the contents thereof, and the same are true to my knowledge, except those matters therein which are stated to be alleged upon information and belief, and as to those matters I believe them to be true. My belief, as to those matters therein not stated upon knowledge, is based upon facts, records, and other pertinent information contained in my files.
The reason this verification is made by me and not Plaintiff is that Plaintiff is not presently in the county wherein the attorneys for the plaintiff maintain their offices.
I certify pursuant to 22 NYCRR 130-1.1(c) that the contentions contained in the within pleadings are not frivolous. Peter G. Barrett
that the within is a (certified) true copy of an order duly entered in the office of the clerk of the within named court on [date] 202[year].

that an order of which the within is a true copy will be presented for settlement to the HON. [judge_name], one of the judges of the within named Court, at [court_address] on 202[year] at [time] O’clock [AM/PM].
